steps.action: The Tsamikos (Greek: Τσάμικος, Tsamikos) or Klef with pivot turn, partner opposite, grounded.
steps.text: The Tsamikos (Greek: Τσάμικος, Tsamikos) or Klef with pivot turn, partner opposite, grounded.

steps.action: The dancers hold each other from each other's ha
steps.text: The dancers hold each other from each other's hands, bent 90 degrees upwards at the elbows.

steps.action: The steps are relatively easy but have to be pre
steps.text: The steps are relatively easy but have to be precise and strictly on beat.

steps.action: The dancer might even stomp his foot in response
steps.text: The dancer might even stomp his foot in response to a strong beat.

steps.action: There is some improvisation involved and many va
steps.text: There is some improvisation involved and many variations of the steps, depending on which area the dancers come from.

steps.action: Over time the dance has taken on many variations
steps.text: Over time the dance has taken on many variations.

steps.action: Its alternative name Kleftikos literally means d
steps.text: Its alternative name Kleftikos literally means dance of the Klephts, because it was associated with the klephts who fought during the Greek War of Independence (1821–1830).

steps.action: Today Tsamiko is a popular dance in festivals an
steps.text: Today Tsamiko is a popular dance in festivals and weddings, especially in the rural areas of Central Greece, Peloponnese, Thessaly, as well as Epirus, where a slower version is performed.

steps.action: Sometimes the dancers dress in the traditional G
steps.text: Sometimes the dancers dress in the traditional Greek fustanella.

steps.action: This dance is usually performed to celebrate nat
steps.text: This dance is usually performed to celebrate national events like in the anniversary of the declaration of the start of Greek War of Independence in 1821.

steps.action: The Tsamiko of Central Greece (Roumeli) is regar
steps.text: The Tsamiko of Central Greece (Roumeli) is regarded as the standard version of this dancing genre and is popular throughout Greece.

steps.action: In Central Greece Tsamiko is the most popular fo
steps.text: In Central Greece Tsamiko is the most popular folk dance.

steps.action: In Peloponnese Tsamiko is one of the most popula
steps.text: In Peloponnese Tsamiko is one of the most popular folk genres together with Syrtos.

steps.action: The Epirote dances Kleftes and Perdikomata have
steps.text: The Epirote dances Kleftes and Perdikomata have been sometimes regarded as variants of Tsamiko, although this is not universally accepted since they have been also classified as distinct genres.

steps.action: A general classification of the dancing genres i
steps.text: A general classification of the dancing genres in Zagori region of Epirus can classify Osmanatakas and Berati as variations of Tsamiko too.

steps.action: Although one of the main dancing genres in Epiru
steps.text: Although one of the main dancing genres in Epirus, Tsamiko is not the most popular folk genre in local discography.

steps.action: The definition of "true" Tsamiko is obscure due
steps.text: The definition of "true" Tsamiko is obscure due to the many regional, choreographic, musical variations, versions, and names of the dance.

steps.action: In the past, it was danced exclusively by men, b
steps.text: In the past, it was danced exclusively by men, but in modern times both men and women take part.

steps.action: Swiss musicologist Samuel Baud-Bovy noted that t
steps.text: Swiss musicologist Samuel Baud-Bovy noted that the Greek Tsamiko was not danced among Cham Albanians.

steps.action: Tsamiko is danced almost exclusively by men.
steps.text: Tsamiko is danced almost exclusively by men.

steps.action: The Tsamiko is known as the Arvanitikos in the r
steps.text: The Tsamiko is known as the Arvanitikos in the region of Mani.

steps.action: In Epirus Tsamiko is especially popular in the r
steps.text: In Epirus Tsamiko is especially popular in the regions of Ioannina and Thesprotia.

steps.action: In general the Tsamiko of Epirus retains a slowe
steps.text: In general the Tsamiko of Epirus retains a slower tempo compared to the Tsamiko of Central Greece.

steps.action: Η Ρυθμική Συγκρότηση της Μουσικής της Ηπείρου.
steps.text: Η Ρυθμική Συγκρότηση της Μουσικής της Ηπείρου.
